corrections.original_claim: Use importc with callconv: cdecl for C APIs unless the library explicitly uses a different calling convention.
corrections.correction: Use `cdecl` on individual imported C procs. Reserve `callconv: cdecl` for pragma blocks such as `{.push callconv: cdecl.}`.

corrections.original_claim: For system-installed libs (e.g., curl), do not copy DLLs; rely on environment variables (LD_LIBRARY_PATH, DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH, PATH) for runtime resolution.
corrections.correction: For system-installed libraries, do not bundle copies next to the executable. Rely on the platform's normal system loader configuration and package-managed install location. Environment variables such as LD_LIBRARY_PATH or PATH are optional overrides, not the default rule.

uncovered_topics: Actual dynlib loading at runtime with a custom shared library
Nim config file (nims) integration for linker flags
needs_stronger_tests: C21 - Add a relocation-specific test that proves an absolute/build-tree rpath breaks after moving the executable

claims.claim_text: Use importc with cdecl for C APIs unless the library explicitly uses a different calling convention.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. `cdecl` on imported C procs compiles and runs with real stdlib functions. `callconv: cdecl` on an individual proc declaration is rejected.

claims.claim_text: Represent opaque C handles as 'type Name = ptr object' types in Nim.
claims.evaluation_notes: ptr OpaqueObj pattern compiles. Also verified with real FILE* API. DETERMINISTIC.

claims.claim_text: For partial or opaque C structs, use incompleteStruct to avoid size/layout mismatches.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The test covers both a field-bearing partial-prefix imported struct and a truly opaque imported struct, and it verifies that `sizeof` on an `incompleteStruct` import fails to compile.

claims.claim_text: For value structs that Nim must pass by value, use bycopy.
claims.evaluation_notes: bycopy struct compiles and can be passed by value to procs. DETERMINISTIC.

claims.claim_text: Declare the C header in the binding (header: '<...>') when the compiler needs definitions.
claims.evaluation_notes: header pragma with stdlib.h resolves C symbols correctly. DETERMINISTIC.

claims.claim_text: For system libraries, link with -l<name> only; do not hardcode -L paths when the OS toolchain can locate them.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. A real system-library binding to libm links and runs with `-lm` only and no explicit `-L` path.

claims.claim_text: For local/third-party libraries, add -L<dir> plus -l<name> (or the platform import library on Windows).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. A local shared library built under a repo-relative directory links successfully via `-L<dir> -l<name>`.

claims.claim_text: For local/third-party libs, use repository-relative paths (e.g., third_party/...) to keep builds hermetic.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The local-library test uses a repo-relative `tests/.../third_party/...` path for the linker input.

claims.claim_text: For local/third-party libs (e.g., PDFium), colocate the shared library next to the executable at runtime.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The shared library is copied next to the compiled test binary and runtime resolution succeeds from that colocated location.

claims.claim_text: For system-installed libs (e.g., curl), do not copy DLLs; rely on environment variables (LD_LIBRARY_PATH, DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH, PATH) for runtime resolution.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. The system-library test runs without bundling any shared library copy. The supported rule is: rely on the platform's normal system loader configuration; env vars are optional overrides, not the default mechanism.

claims.claim_text: On Linux, add rpath only for tests/apps that load colocated shared libs: --passL:"-Wl,-rpath,\$ORIGIN".
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The test binary uses `{.passL: "-Wl,-rpath,\\$ORIGIN".}` and `readelf -d` confirms `RUNPATH [$ORIGIN]`.

claims.claim_text: CI is the authoritative spec for supported platforms, toolchains, and flags. Any local workflow not compatible with CI is disallowed.
claims.evaluation_notes: Process/policy claim.

claims.claim_text: Ensure the CI toolchain and the dependency toolchain match (e.g., MSVC + vcpkg x64-windows-release with --cc:vcc).
claims.evaluation_notes: CI/policy claim.

claims.claim_text: On macOS, resolve include/link paths via staticExec("brew --prefix <formula>").
claims.evaluation_notes: Platform-specific, not testable on Linux.

claims.claim_text: On Windows, use MSVC via --cc:vcc. Prefer vcpkg only; avoid Chocolatey and MSYS2.
claims.evaluation_notes: Windows-specific.

claims.claim_text: On Windows CI with vcpkg: export VCPKG_ROOT to the installed triplet root and add its bin directory to PATH for runtime DLL resolution.
claims.evaluation_notes: Windows CI-specific.

claims.claim_text: On Windows, do not rely on implicit -l<name> for MSVC; use .lib/.dll.lib paths instead.
claims.evaluation_notes: Windows MSVC-specific.

claims.claim_text: Do not mix toolchains on Windows (MSVC + MSYS2/MinGW) or switch package managers midstream.
claims.evaluation_notes: Policy claim.

claims.claim_text: For vendored libs, always prefer repository-relative paths under third_party/.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The local-library test uses a vendored-style repo-relative path under `third_party/`.

claims.claim_text: Do not rely on environment variables for runtime discovery of vendored/local shared libs; colocate them instead.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The colocated shared-library test runs without any extra environment-variable setup.

claims.claim_text: Do not use build-tree-only rpaths or absolute paths to non-system shared libraries.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. External `readelf -d` inspection of the compiled test binary showed `RUNPATH [$ORIGIN]` rather than an embedded build-tree path. This supports the rule, but the repo does not yet include a relocation-failure reproduction for an absolute/build-tree rpath.

claims.claim_text: The CI workflow (ci.yml) uses jiro4989/setup-nim-action@v2 with stable Nim version.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed by reading references/ci.yml.

claims.claim_text: The CI workflow uses atlas install for Nim dependency management.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed.

claims.claim_text: The release workflow produces per-platform archives: .tar.gz for Linux/macOS, .zip for Windows.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed by reading references/release.yml.

claims.claim_text: The release workflow creates a draft GitHub Release via softprops/action-gh-release@v2.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed.

claims.claim_text: The Windows CI uses johnwason/vcpkg-action@v7 with triplet x64-windows-release.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed.

claims.claim_text: The Linux release strips the executable (strip <package>).
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed.

claims.claim_text: The macOS release does not strip the executable.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed by absence of strip step in macOS job.

claims.claim_text: Incompatible on macOS: relying on DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH or full-path linking to a .dylib.
claims.evaluation_notes: Platform-specific, not testable on Linux.

claims.claim_text: Keep test builds simple and reproducible: compile, then run, with minimal environment mutation.
claims.evaluation_notes: Process guideline.

claims.claim_text: The release workflow Windows step copies VCPKG_ROOT\bin\*.dll next to the executable.
claims.evaluation_notes: CI YAML content — confirmed (Copy-Item $env:VCPKG_ROOT\bin\*.dll).

failure_samples.summary: No-skill worker bypassed the vendored shared-library path by compiling the fixture C sources directly instead of linking the built helper .so.
No-skill worker added a wrapper layer around snapshot_read instead of binding the C API surface directly.
failure_samples.evidence: proc benchffi_snapshot_read*(handle: BenchffiHandle): BenchffiSnapshot = benchffi_snapshot_read_out(handle, addr result)
